ddls:
CREATE TABLE "passengers" (
  "passenger_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each passenger */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the passenger */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Gender of the passenger (male/female) */,
  "age" REAL /* Age of the passenger */,
  "pclass" INTEGER /* Passenger class (1st, 2nd, or 3rd) */,
  "ticket" TEXT /* Ticket number */,
  "fare" REAL /* Fare paid for the ticket */,
  "embarked" TEXT /* Port of embarkation (C, Q, S) */,
  "survived" INTEGER /* Survival status (0 = No, 1 = Yes) */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the passenger */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the passenger */,
  "is_child" INTEGER /* Whether the passenger is a child or not */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("passenger_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "passenger_families" (
  "family_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the family relationship */,
  "passenger_id" INTEGER /* ID of the passenger in the family relationship */,
  "relation_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship (e.g., sibling, parent) */,
  "relation_type_description" TEXT /* Description of the relationship type */,
  "family_member_id" INTEGER /* ID of the family member */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("family_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_passenger_families_passenger_id FOREIGN KEY ("passenger_id") REFERENCES passengers ("passenger_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tickets" (
  "ticket_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the ticket */,
  "ticket_number" TEXT /* Ticket number */,
  "fare" REAL /* Fare paid for the ticket */,
  "passenger_id" INTEGER /* ID of the passenger who purchased the ticket */,
  "purchase_date" TEXT /* Date when the ticket was purchased */,
  "cabin_number" TEXT /* Cabin number assigned to the passenger */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ticket_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tickets_passenger_id FOREIGN KEY ("passenger_id") REFERENCES passengers ("passenger_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "embarkation_ports" (
  "port_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each port */,
  "port_code" TEXT /* Code for the port (C, Q, S) */,
  "port_name" TEXT /* Full name of the port */,
  "port_location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the port */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("port_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "survival_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the survival statistic record */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the transportation incident */,
  "incident_date" TEXT /* Date of the transportation incident */,
  "pclass" INTEGER /* Passenger class (1st, 2nd, 3rd) */,
  "total_passengers" INTEGER /* Total number of passengers in this class */,
  "survivors" INTEGER /* Number of survivors from this class */,
  "survival_rate" REAL /* Survival rate calculated as survivors/total_passengers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survival_statistics_pclass FOREIGN KEY ("pclass") REFERENCES passengers ("pclass")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the user role (e.g., analyst, admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* List of permissions associated with this role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role's responsibilities and permissions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the account */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user account security */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "last_login_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active or not */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access event */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_device" TEXT /* Device used to access the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
